papers.title: SkillLens: Adaptive Multi-Granularity Skill Reuse for Cost-Efficient LLM Agents
papers.innovation: Introduces adaptive multi-granularity skill reuse to optimize cost and relevance for LLM agents. This approach tackles the practical challenge of managing complex skill libraries by allowing agents to select skills at appropriate levels of detail, avoiding costly and irrelevant invocations.
papers.benchmarks: Likely improved cost-efficiency (e.g., token usage) and sustained or enhanced task performance for LLM agents across various procedural tasks compared to flat skill libraries.
papers.use_case: Building cost-effective and scalable LLM agents that can efficiently reuse learned skills across a wide range of tasks, particularly in scenarios where computational resources are a concern or task complexity varies.

papers.title: CoCoDA: Co-evolving Compositional DAG for Tool-Augmented Agents
papers.innovation: Proposes a framework for co-evolving compositional DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs) that represent tool libraries alongside the agent's planner. This addresses the critical engineering challenge of scaling tool-augmented agents by ensuring efficient retrieval and adaptation of tools as the library grows.
papers.benchmarks: Expected improvements in tool retrieval efficiency, scalability of tool libraries, and robust performance of tool-augmented agents on complex tasks with a growing set of available tools.
papers.use_case: Developing advanced, scalable tool-augmented LLM agents that need to manage, evolve, and efficiently utilize a large and growing repertoire of external tools and subroutines, crucial for complex real-world applications.

papers.title: Spatial Priming Outperforms Semantic Prompting: A Grid-Based Approach to Improving LLM Accuracy on Chart Data Extraction
papers.innovation: Introduces 'Spatial Priming' and a 'Grid-Based Approach' to significantly improve the accuracy of multimodal LLMs in extracting data from scientific charts. This offers a concrete and effective method to overcome challenges with non-standardized chart data, outperforming traditional semantic prompting.
papers.benchmarks: Demonstrates superior accuracy in data extraction from various scientific charts, particularly non-standardized ones, compared to existing prompting strategies like semantic prompting.
papers.use_case: Automating the accurate extraction of numerical and categorical data from images of scientific charts, graphs, and plots. This is highly valuable for large-scale scientific literature analysis, data digitization, and enhancing data-driven research workflows.
